title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Study finds sharp decline in Black, Hispanic researchers receiving NIH funding
items.description: Drop in those receiving grants and fellowships in 2025 shows impact of Trump administration rollbacks to diversity initiatives

items.title: Will the World Cup kick off disease outbreaks?
items.description: Don’t expect pathogens to steal the show, says research group that studied the risk of visiting fans spreading infections

items.title: First results put neutrino experiment in China on track for breakthrough
items.description: JUNO could beat much bigger and more expensive rivals to order neutrinos by mass

items.title: ‘Weird and capricious’: Experts struggle to understand new list of political jobs at science agencies
items.description: NIH programs and leadership are hit especially hard, while other science agencies are barely mentioned

items.title: Immense whale ‘graveyard’ discovered in the deep sea
items.description: Stretching 1200 kilometers, the unusual collection of carcasses includes fossils older than 5 million years

items.title: This scientist is rethinking how to put GPS collars on wildlife
items.description: Richard Bischof’s devices aim to make telemetry research cheaper, higher quality, and more humane

items.title: How can you tell whether your dog is right-pawed or left-pawed?
items.description: <cite>Science</cite> chats with researchers about a new method of assessing canine “handedness” and what it reveals about your pup’s health and personality

items.title: ‘Working without them is painful’: WHO’s Ebola chief reflects on U.S. colleagues’ absence
items.description: Chikwe Ihekweazu, on the ground in Congo, discusses challenges of bringing the outbreak under control

items.title: Canadian biologists decry plans to bypass environmental reviews for infrastructure projects
items.description: Government proposal to speed up development could harm protected plants and animals, scientists say

items.title: Misconduct sleuth in China swiftly gains acclaim for calling out questionable papers
items.description: Independent vlogger Geng Hongwei galvanizes often-sluggish institutions to investigate and punish prominent scientists
